segments.text: If you have seen Japanese sharpeners,
there is no chance you aren't obsessed
about them. Like this Tigal sharpener.
See, most sharpeners give you no choice.
But this comes with a built-in dial with
five different settings. You can adjust
the length and angle of a lead with a
simple click. Whether you need a short,
sturdy point or a long sharp needle, one
tool handles it all. But even with a
perfect angle, sharpening is a literal
pain in the wrist. Twist, release,
repeat. So for that, we have Sonic
Ratcheta. It uses a built-in ratchet
mechanism just like a socket wrench. You
don't have to let go of the pencil. Just
twist back and forth. It makes
sharpening faster, easier, and oddly
satisfying to hear that clicking sound.
It's cool, but time for the big daddy.
Now, the toga return. In sharpening, the
biggest problem is not the motion. It's
knowing when to stop. We all oversharpen
until the lead snaps. This one actually
detects when the tip is perfect and
literally ejects the pencil so that you
can't waste another millimeter. This is
some cool design.
